note: Each row runs a fresh headless session with only based-claudius loaded (bare mode) and records which plugin skills the model invoked via the Skill tool. Hard rows assert a contract and fail the suite; advisory rows measure probabilistic auto-invocation of workflow skills and only surface in the report. Detection reads model-initiated Skill tool_use blocks; user-typed slash commands are expanded pre-model and are out of scope here.

rows.prompt: We always use pnpm, never npm, in this repo. Remember that permanently as a durable memory card.
rows.why: The memory skill sets disable-model-invocation: true. The model must NOT auto-invoke it even for a memory-shaped request; durable memory stays user-triggered. A firing here is a real governance regression.

rows.prompt: What is the capital of France?
rows.why: Off-domain prompt. No workflow skill should activate on general knowledge questions.

rows.prompt: Explain in one sentence what a hash map is.
rows.why: Conceptual question with no repository action. Skills should stay quiet.

rows.prompt: Implement a function that reverses a linked list in src/list.js and add a test for it.
rows.why: A concrete implementation request is the router skill's stated trigger. Auto-invocation of a workflow router is probabilistic, so this is advisory: the model may also just implement directly.

rows.prompt: Run the checks on my current changes and tell me if this is ready to merge.
rows.why: Validation/review request maps to the check skill (or the router). Advisory for the same reason.

rows.prompt: Before we touch any code, plan how we would migrate this project from CommonJS to ES modules.
rows.why: Explicit plan-first request maps to the plan skill. Advisory.
